LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT COLINDRES & ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. WHILE CALIFORNIA HAS A LIBERAL POLICY FAVORING ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS, THAT POLICY IS INSUFFICIENT TO WARRANT THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION DUE TO THE UNDUE PREJUDICE THAT WOULD BE SUFFERED BY PLAINTIFF MERRILEE HARRIS IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PERMITTING THE FILING OF THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT WOULD INEVITABLY RESULT IN A LENGTHY CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL AND WOULD REQUIRE MS. HARRIS TO PARTICIPATE IN GREATLY INCREASED DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS. C&A IS NOT PREJUDICED BY THE DENIAL OF THIS MOTION SINCE ITS INDEMNITY CLAIMS CAN BE ALLEGED IN A SEPARATE LAWSUIT AND ARE NOT EVEN RIPE UNLESS AND UNTIL THERE IS A JUDGMENT AGAINST C&A. C&A'S ARGUMENT THAT THE RECENT ADDITIONS TO MS. HARRIS'S COMPLAINT REQUIRES THAT C&A BE ABLE TO CROSS-CLAIM AGAINST A HOST OF SUBCONTRACTORS IS UNPERSUASIVE. COUNSEL FOR MS. HARRIS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Defendant Collindres & Associates' Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Collindres & Associates' Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint.

Cross-complainant Colindres & Associates, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a second amended cross-complaint is denied. While California has a liberal policy favoring allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy is insufficient to warrant the granting of this motion due to the undue prejudice that would be suffered by plaintiff Merrilee Harris if the motion is granted. Permitting the filing of the second amended cross-complaint would inevitably result in a lengthy continuance of the trial and would require Ms. Harris to participate in greatly increased discovery proceedings. C&A is not prejudiced by the denial of this motion since its indemnity claims can be alleged in a separate lawsuit and are not even ripe unless and until there is a judgment against C&A. C&A's argument that the recent additions to Ms. Harris's complaint requires that C&A be able to cross-claim against a host of subcontractors is unpersuasive.

Counsel for Ms. Harris is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey T, Makoff, Esq. of Valle Makoff LLC, 415.328.0484, appearing for the Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Merrilee Harris;
Michael A. Walker, Esq. Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, 415.262.8530, appearing for the Defendant/Cross-Complainant Colindres & Associates, Inc.

PLAINTIFF MERRILEE HARRIS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is ARGUED.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff Merrilee Harris' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted in part and defendant Colindres & Associates, Inc.'s request for a trial continuance is denied. Ms. Harris' proposed new claims (the abuse of process, injunction and a portion of her declaratory relief claims) alleging invalidity of the mechanic's lien and lis pendens are not actionable and thus she is denied leave to amend those claims. The filing of a mechanic's lien and a lis pendens are absolutely privileged. Ms. Harris is granted leave to allege her proposed new section 17200 claim and the portion of her proposed new declaratory relief claim not alleging invalidity of the mechanic's lien and lis pendens because those claims have at least arguable merit and the policy of California is to liberally allow amendments to pleadings at any stage of the case. C&A has not shown that there is a need for a trial continuance. Order signed. (see order for full details)
